title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Since its (0) INCEPTION (INCEPT), cinema has been a powerful medium for (17) STORYTELLING (STORY). It combines (18) VISUAL (VISION) arts with music and performance to create an (19) UNFORGETTABLE (FORGET) experience. Filmmakers use (20) IMAGINATIVE (IMAGINE) techniques to transport audiences to different worlds. The (21) POPULARITY (POPULAR) of blockbuster movies (22) ENSURES (SURE) that the industry remains (23) FINANCIALLY (FINANCE) viable. However, independent films often provide more (24) THOUGHTFUL (THOUGHT) explorations of social issues. By reflecting the diversity of human experience, cinema fosters empathy and understanding across cultures. Whether through laughter or tears, movies have the unique ability to connect people, sparking conversations that transcend borders. As technology continues to evolve, from silent black-and-white films to immersive virtual reality, the core essence of cinema remains the same: the desire to share stories that resonate with the heart and mind, inspiring us to see the world through a different lens and appreciate the beauty of our shared humanity.

example.text: Since its (0) INCEPTION (INCEPT), cinema has been...
example.explanation: Noun required after the possessive 'its'; INCEPT → INCEPTION.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'arts'; VISION → VISUAL.
Adjective with a negative prefix required; FORGET → UNFORGETTABLE.
Adjective required to modify 'techniques'; IMAGINE → IMAGINATIVE.
Noun required as the subject; POPULAR → POPULARITY.
Verb in present simple third person required; SURE → ENSURES.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'viable'; FINANCE → FINANCIALLY.
